title: KO live capture broken: LiveScoreSync hardcodes group-stage id in FIFA detail URL
description: ROOT CAUSE (confirmed prod 2026-06-28): Workers.LiveScoreSync @detail_base = https://api.fifa.com/api/v3/live/football/17/285023/289273 hardcodes 289273 = the GROUP stage. FIFA live path is /17/285023/{stageId}/{matchId} and EVERY knockout round has its own stageId (group=289273, R32=289287, R16/QF/SF/F each different — carried in each rounds.json match's matchcentreUrl). So all KO live-detail fetches return {:ok,200,nil} (null body) -> decoder needs is_map -> skipped -> is_live never set, no live capture. Group matches captured fine all tournament (289273 correct for them); first-ever KO (South Africa v Canada, 28 Jun, fifaId 400021518 — CORRECT id per rounds.json) hit wrong stage and never captured (settled 0-1 via openfootball only). PROVEN: fetch 400021518 under 289273 -> nil; under 289287 -> MatchStatus 0, 0-1, South Africa. This is the 'first KO capture' hco WS1 was meant to verify on 28 Jun — it failed. FIX: add fixtures.fifa_stage_id (nullable); LiveIds/KnockoutIds parse stage from matchcentreUrl when backfilling fifa_match_id (fill even if id already set, to backfill existing R32); LiveScoreSync builds .../285023/#{stage||289273}/#{matchid}. Affects all remaining KO matches (next: Brazil v Japan 29 Jun 18:00). SA v Canada live timeline unrecoverable (finished).
notes: DEPLOYED v0.11.22 2026-06-28 ~21:55 UTC + PROD-VERIFIED. rpc LiveIds.assign -> %{assigned:16, by_stage:16, errors:0} (all 16 R32 stages backfilled). Brazil v Japan fifa_stage_id=289287; /detail fetch under it = populated frame (MatchStatus 1, home=Brazil) — was null pre-fix. Mechanism proven end-to-end. REMAINING to close: confirm first real live KO capture (Brazil v Japan 29 Jun 18:00 flips is_live).

title: Live capture stops at fixed kickoff+210min window — weather break truncates a match
description: France v Iraq (match 400021492, 2026-06-22): a ~2h half-time weather suspension (FIFA MatchStatus 11) pushed the match end past kickoff+210min. LiveScoreSync.in_window and clear_stuck_live were both anchored on the fixed kickoff+210min horizon, so capture stopped mid-second-half (last frame 66'/3-0 at kickoff+209.6min) and is_live was force-cleared while the match was still in play (74', MatchStatus 3) — losing late goals + the final score from the buzz/replay timeline. Fix: in_window keeps capturing while is_live (weather-proof, follows liveness not a clock); clear_stuck_live time backstop decoupled to @abandon_min=360 so it can't race the live-extended window; openfootball :completed stays the primary self-heal.

title: e5o fills reverted by Ingest two-writer overwrite — team-identity no-downgrade guard
description: Shipped e5o (v0.11.19) fills R32 placeholder slots from FIFA, but the fills did not stick: Fixture @castable includes team1/team2 and Ingest casts openfootball's parsed team names on every ResultSync (15-min). openfootball still publishes the bracket PLACEHOLDER (e.g. USA team2='3B/E/F/I/J'), so Ingest reverted e5o's filled real name ('Bosnia & Herzegovina') back to the placeholder — preserve_settled only guarded RESULT fields (iy1), not team identity. e5o fills every 10min, Ingest reverts every 15min → flicker, mostly reverted ('still failing to match FIFA'). ROOT CAUSE confirmed via failing test (e5o assign fills team2='France', placeholder re-sync reverts to '2B'). FIX: preserve_resolved_teams/2 guard in Ingest — team identity is monotonic (placeholder→real only); openfootball keeps real→real authority; placeholder-over-real dropped. End-to-end regression proves e5o fills AND Ingest preserves AND real→real still writes. 566 tests green. Diagnosed via systematic-debugging; advisor-reviewed (approach approved; flagged that prod e5o-execution is still unconfirmed — verify a third-placed slot sticks >15min post-deploy + the 'knockout team backfill:' log line).
notes: VERIFIED IN PROD (v0.11.20, 2026-06-27 ~18:30). Parsed /bracket R32 slot ids (r32-74-away, r32-81-away — actual bracket slots, NOT group tables): fixture 74 Germany v Paraguay (was 3A/B/C/D/F) + fixture 81 USA v Bosnia & Herzegovina (was 3B/E/F/I/J) both FILLED by e5o and HELD for 20+ min across ResultSync cycles → the no-downgrade guard makes fills stick. These are exactly the 2 anchored thirds FIFA has resolved; remaining '3rd ·' slots correctly pending (FIFA-unresolved or both-placeholder anchored-only). Confound closed (slot ids, not page-wide grep). Fix confirmed end-to-end.
close_reason: Closed

title: Prediction writes broadcast fixtures:changed so live dashboards re-pull (stale-board fix)
notes: Found while scoping 0ft/a4j (predictex-4ez follow-up). Only result-settle paths (Ingest, FifaFallback, LiveScore) called Tournament.broadcast_change/0; the 4 prediction writers (create_prediction, admin_upsert_prediction, admin_save_round_predictions, save_round_predictions) did NOT. Reachable bug: an admin entering/importing a prediction on an already-completed fixture (admin_upsert has no lockout; import writes past rounds) changes standings, but no broadcast fired — open /predictions sessions (which subscribe via 9p0) stayed stale until the next result settle. FIX: a private broadcast_on_success/1 helper wraps all 4 writers; only {:ok,_} broadcasts (failed/locked writes don't). Coarse-by-design (over-broadcast = cheap re-pull). title: FIFA-capture result fallback: provisionally settle a fixture when openfootball lags
description:
PROBLEM (exposed live by France v Iraq 2026-06-22): openfootball is the result authority but it lagged — the match was over (3-0, confirmed) yet stayed status :scheduled / unscored for hours; settled manually via admin override. We already capture the authoritative FIFA final via the live feed, so automate it as a fallback.

DESIGN (openfootball stays PRIMARY; FIFA capture is a PROVISIONAL fallback):
- A sweep (in Workers.ResultSync or a sibling) finds fixtures past kickoff, status != :completed, with no openfootball result, AND a captured FIFA *finished* frame (MatchStatus 0) carrying a final score (Capture.latest_detail_body/1 + LiveScore.attrs_from_body/2 already decode this). Settle provisionally: status :completed + home/away goals from the FIFA finished frame.
- Provenance: add result_source enum (:openfootball | :fifa | :admin). openfootball reconciliation OVERWRITES a :fifa-provisional when it later publishes (its @replace_on_conflict upsert already does score/status); an :admin override must NOT be silently overwritten by the FIFA fallback (precedence admin > openfootball > fifa-provisional, with openfootball as the authority that confirms/corrects fifa).
- PREREQUISITE: predictex-ius (the weather-break capture fix) — without it the finished MatchStatus-0 frame is lost for a delayed match, so the fallback has nothing to read. With it, the finished frame is reliably captured.
- SCOPE v1: group stage only (regulation score = final), mirroring p4o. Knockouts need ET/pens handling (regulation goals = Period in {3,5}; defer, gate on the knockout work / predictex-uyf).
- UI: surface provisional (FIFA) vs confirmed (openfootball) on the board/recap so it is auditable.

Realises docs/plan.md's 'near-live fallback' intent, but using our own FIFA capture instead of an external worldcupapi.com dependency.
close_reason: Closed

title: KO fixture identity: key openfootball ingest on stable match num (resolve teams in place, no duplicates)
description:
BLOCKS predictex-hco WS1 (fifa_match_id backfill). ROOT CAUSE (verified 2026-06-20 vs live openfootball 2026/worldcup.json): the 32 knockout fixtures carry bracket-PLACEHOLDER teams (team1='2A','1A'..'3X'; first KO = Round of 32, num 73, 2A v 2B, 2026-06-28 19:00Z). predictex identifies fixtures by external_ref = 'date team1 v team2' (Ingest upsert conflict_target = :external_ref). When the bracket resolves after the final group match, openfootball flips '2A'->'Brazil' on the SAME match (stable openfootball 'num' field, 73..104 for KO), but predictex's external_ref CHANGES -> no conflict match -> the automatic ResultSync (*/5... actually 15-min cron) INSERTS a duplicate fixture, orphaning the placeholder (and any predictions/captures on it). Also blocks Fifa.LiveIds.assign, which joins FIFA rounds.json <-> fixture on {date, team-name set} and can't match a placeholder-team row.

FIX (automation, not a manual ops pause): give fixtures a STABLE identity from openfootball's match 'num' so team resolution updates IN PLACE.
- Migration: add fixtures.source_num :integer + unique index (nullable; Postgres NULLs don't conflict).
- Openfootball.parse_match/1: emit num.
- Ingest.upsert_fixture: identify existing fixture by source_num (fallback to external_ref on first sync before num is set), update in place incl. new teams + new external_ref; else insert. Self-backfills num on the first post-deploy sync (104/104), then resolution is a same-row update.
- Tests: placeholder->resolved match (num 73, 2A v 2B -> real teams) updates the SAME fixture id, predictions intact, no duplicate; existing group-stage idempotency preserved.

Then predictex-hco WS1 backfill (Fifa.LiveIds.assign) runs cleanly post-resolution. Deadline: land before first KO 2026-06-28. Scope: ingest/openfootball/fixture schema only (does NOT touch scoring, live capture window [done, @post_min 210], or the c46 KO picks-reveal UI [separate, hco WS4]).
notes:
VERIFIED vs live worldcup.json (2026-06-20) + advisor review — design confirmed with 2 refinements:

1. SCOPE: 'num' is present+UNIQUE on all 32 KO matches (73-104) but ABSENT on all 72 group matches (they have no num key; group teams never change so external_ref is already stable). So: KO fixtures key on source_num; group keeps external_ref. source_num is KO-only (group rows stay NULL — fine, Postgres unique index allows multiple NULLs). NOT '104/104 get num' as first stated.

2. DEADLINE is tighter than 'before first KO 28 Jun': must deploy BEFORE THE FINAL GROUP MATCH, with ≥1 sync cycle of lead. Why: the automatic 15-min ResultSync creates the duplicate the moment openfootball flips 2A->Brazil (right after the bracket resolves at group-stage end). g8m must already be deployed AND have run one sync to stamp source_num onto the still-placeholder KO rows (bootstrap-by-ref) BEFORE the flip — then resolution is an in-place same-row update. Deploy after the flip = inherit duplicates + a cleanup job.

3. SAFE: get_fixture_by_ref has no prod consumer treating external_ref as a stable handle (only ingest_test lookups); predictions key on fixture_id. So external_ref may safely mutate on KO resolution.

IMPL (app-level upsert, replaces DB on_conflict): find existing by source_num (KO, when set) else by external_ref (bootstrap/group); update-in-place via changeset(parsed_attrs) else insert. Parsed attrs carry no cohort_*_pct, so the changeset-update naturally preserves admin cohort % (the existing 're-sync preserves cohort' test is the canary — must stay green). Drop @replace_on_conflict. Add Tournament.get_fixture_by_source_num/1 + fixtures.source_num column + unique index.
CODE-COMPLETE + REVIEWED, committed LOCAL (4e6ffa9), unpushed. TDD: parse_match emits source_num; migration (fixtures.source_num + unique index); Ingest find_fixture (KO by source_num, group by external_ref, ref-fallback bootstrap) replaces DB on_conflict; removed @replace_on_conflict (two-writer rule now preserved because changeset-update casts only parsed attrs, which exclude cohort_*_pct + FIFA-owned live_*/fifa_match_id). Independent opus review: Ready-with-fixes, no Critical. Applied its Important (added a test pinning live_*/fifa_match_id/cohort survival through the source_num resolution branch — the named regression was previously unpinned since the cohort canary only hit the ref path) + Minor (find-then-write atomicity comment; backstopped by both unique indexes). 408 tests green.

DEPLOY PRECONDITION (dominant risk): g8m must be DEPLOYED + run >=1 ResultSync cycle while the 32 KO rows are still placeholders, BEFORE the bracket flips at the final group match. Deploy after the flip = get_fixture_by_source_num nil + get_fixture_by_ref(resolved_ref) nil -> INSERT, recreating the duplicate. (Note: get_by on source_num/external_ref can't raise MultipleResultsError — both are unique-indexed — so the reviewer's MultipleResultsError concern is moot; the timing guard is the real point.) Bundles with the pending v0.11.10 deploy (9p0): tagging v0.11.10 deploys 9p0+g8m together, migration runs via Release.migrate. Plenty of lead (~8 days to final group match). UNBLOCKS hco WS1 (Fifa.LiveIds.assign can then join on resolved teams).
DEPLOYED in v0.11.10 (2026-06-20: Deploy success; migration 20260620023346 AddSourceNumToFixtures applied in prod — 'create index fixtures_source_num_index', migrated 0.0s; /health 200). The next ResultSync (15-min cron) bootstraps: stamps source_num onto the 32 KO placeholder rows via the ref fallback. Left OPEN — full acceptance (no duplicate on resolution) is verifiable only when the bracket resolves after the final group match; the deploy-before-flip precondition is now SATISFIED (deployed ~8 days ahead with the cron stamping placeholders continuously). UNBLOCKS hco WS1. To verify post-deploy (needs a prod read): confirm all 32 KO fixtures have source_num set after a sync cycle.
POST-DEPLOY VERIFIED 2026-06-21 (prod read): all 32 knockout fixtures have source_num stamped — Repo count {ko_total, with_source_num} = {32, 32}. The 15-min ResultSync ref-fallback bootstrap stamped every KO placeholder row, so the deploy-before-the-flip precondition is satisfied. Remaining: the final no-duplicate confirmation at bracket resolution (when openfootball flips placeholder teams 2A->real and the row must update in place, not insert a dup) — verifiable only after the final group match. Then g8m is fully done.
close_reason: No-dup invariant verified in prod 2026-06-23 at partial bracket resolution: 3 R32 teams (Germany/Mexico/USA) resolved in place via source_num with dup_source_num=[] and dup_external_ref=[] across all 32 KO fixtures. Mechanism proven; remaining slots resolve the same way as the group stage finishes.

title: Dashboard live tick: auto-enable Match Preview at −30 min (no refresh)
description:
On /predictions (MyPredictionsLive), the Match-preview link, lock badge, live scores and recap state are all frozen because @now is captured once at mount. Add a self-paced LiveView timer that re-pulls Dashboard.for_player so they update over the websocket with no page refresh, self-terminating when settled.

## Context
Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-19-dashboard-live-tick-design.md. Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-19-dashboard-live-tick.md. Originating request: LiveView/websocket to auto-enable Match Preview when kickoff <= 30 mins.
design: Pure Dashboard.next_tick_delay/2: 30s while a match is live, exact gap to the next preview-open (kickoff − cta_lead_seconds) or kickoff-lock threshold across all rounds, nil once every fixture completed. MyPredictionsLive schedules Process.send_after(:tick) on connected mount and reschedules from each :tick; nil => stop. Expose Predictions.cta_lead_seconds/0 (DRY the 30-min constant). Preserves @active_ordinal (round-tab selection). Deferred: subscribe dashboard to fifa:snapshots PubSub to drop the 30s score-poll.
acceptance_criteria: next_tick_delay/2 domain test table (live=30s, before/inside preview window, completed/TBC=nil, soonest-across-rounds, sub-second floor=1000ms); LiveView test mutates fixture to live then send(lv.pid,:tick) and asserts re-render shows LIVE/score; gate green (mix precommit).
close_reason: Shipped in v0.11.9 (deployed 2026-06-20: CI Deploy success, /health 200, anon / 200). MyPredictionsLive self-paced :tick re-pulls Dashboard.for_player over the websocket; pure Dashboard.next_tick_delay/2 (30s live / exact gap to next preview-open or kickoff-lock / nil once settled); Predictions.cta_lead_seconds/0 DRYs the 30-min constant; @active_ordinal preserved. Acceptance met: next_tick_delay/2 domain table + send(:tick) re-render LiveView test; gate green (400 passed). Deferred (separate beads): 9p0 (drop 30s poll → fifa:snapshots PubSub).

title: Wire a local pre-commit gate (close the local↔remote seam)
description: Principles review (2026-06-18, against docs/software-delivery-principles.md §4 jidoka + docs/engineering-principles.md §1/§5) found the highest-leverage delivery gap: the 'precommit' mix alias exists (compile --warnings-as-errors, deps.unlock --unused, format, test) but NOTHING runs it — no lefthook.yml, no wired git hook, no --no-verify block. CI's Quality job (.github/workflows/ci-deploy.yml) is the ONLY enforcement point, which is exactly the local↔remote seam §4 forbids: format drift, warnings, unused deps and failing tests can all reach the remote and only fail in CI. Aligns with standing CLAUDE.md prefs (Lefthook primary git-hooks tool; automate-everything; shift-left stops at commit/push). When wiring, ALSO align the alias to CI in the same change: bare 'format' (mutates) -> 'format --check-formatted' (verifies), and 'deps.unlock --unused' -> 'deps.unlock --check-unused' — else local mutates while CI verifies.
acceptance_criteria: lefthook.yml runs 'mix precommit' on pre-commit; precommit alias uses --check-formatted and --check-unused to match CI exactly; a --no-verify bypass is blocked (Claude Code PreToolUse hook or equivalent); README/CLAUDE.md documents the gate.
close_reason: Local pre-commit gate wired and verified. lefthook.yml runs mix precommit on commits staging Elixir files (glob-scoped so beads/docs commits stay fast). precommit alias aligned to CI Quality job (deps.unlock --check-unused, format --check-formatted) — single source, no local-remote seam. Beads owns core.hooksPath, so the gate is invoked from the committed .beads/hooks/pre-commit outside the beads markers (verified bd hooks install preserves it) — propagates via the committed hook, no separate install. Gate-bypass blocked by a Claude Code PreToolUse hook (scripts/guard-no-verify.sh in .claude/settings.json), which strips quoted commit messages so it only matches real CLI flags (7-case test: flag blocks in any position, message-mentions allowed). Verified: gate blocks a mis-formatted staged file; mix precommit green 367 tests. Dev tooling — not a prod deploy.

title: Knockout-readiness for live capture (before first KO 2026-06-28)
description:
Group-stage live capture is fully automatic on v0.11.0 (72/104 fixtures have fifa_match_id; the */5 cron arms Workers.LiveScoreSync per in-window fixture). But the 32 KNOCKOUT fixtures are NOT captured: they have no fifa_match_id (producer query skips fifa_match_id IS NULL) and still carry bracket-placeholder teams (2A, 1C, 1E...). First knockout kicks off 2026-06-28 19:00Z (2A v 2B). TWO prerequisites before then:
1. BACKFILL fifa_match_id for the 32 knockout fixtures. Path: Predictex.Fifa.Reference.fetch_rounds() -> Fifa.LiveIds.assign() (the group-stage backfill path), but it can only run once FIFA publishes the knockout match IDs in rounds.json (after the bracket resolves at end of group stage). Verify rounds.json carries KO IdMatch values, then run assign on prod and confirm all 104 fixtures have fifa_match_id. (Related to predictex-i9k, which is the member-import side.)
2. WIDEN the producer window for extra-time/penalties — predictex-cvx (P2 bug): @post_min 150 truncates KO matches going to ET/pens (~155-185min) so the buzz goes dark and is_live sticks true. Bump @post_min to ~210 and/or add an independent is_live auto-clear sweep BEFORE knockouts.
Acceptance: by 2026-06-28, all knockout fixtures have a fifa_match_id AND the producer window covers ET+pens, verified on the first knockout (fifa_captures gains rows through full time + shootout, is_live clears on finish).
acceptance_criteria: All 104 fixtures have fifa_match_id; producer window covers ET+penalties; first knockout (2026-06-28) captured through shootout with is_live clearing on finish
notes: WS1 BUILT 2026-06-23 (commit 5151a85, committed-local, NOT yet deployed). Self-arming KO fifa_match_id backfill: Workers.KnockoutIds (Oban cron */10) — stop-before-fetch (no KO fixture missing id → no network), else fetch rounds.json + LiveIds.assign, log coverage (KO fifa_match_id: N/32) + name/slot split. LiveIds.plan enhanced: skip-already-assigned (no overwrite); NAME match authoritative (group+KO); KO-fixture-only date+time SLOT fallback (Crosswalk.slot_key {utc_date,hour,minute}) for name-misses — robust to openfootball team-resolution lag; group never slot-matches; slot index sourced only from FIFA KO-stage rounds. Slot key PROXY-VERIFIED: openfootball kickoff_at == FIFA rounds.json date to the minute across all 72 group matches (agree=72, mismatch=0). assign/1 now returns %{assigned,by_name,by_slot,errors}. Reviewed (code-reviewer sonnet): SPEC ✅/Approved, 4 minors folded in. 494 tests green. REMAINING: (1) DEPLOY before 28 Jun (worker only runs in prod once deployed; harmless poll until FIFA publishes KO matches, then self-arms). (2) VERIFY on 28 Jun: KO rounds populate in rounds.json → worker assigns → KO fifa_match_id 32/32 (104/104 total) → first KO captured through ET/pens, is_live clears on finish. WS2 already covered (cvx + ius). Build doc/design: hco bead notes + this session.

title: LiveScoreSync window @post_min 150 truncates knockout ET/penalties -> buzz dark + is_live stuck
description: Whole-branch review of predictex-rfm (auto-capture) found: the producer Workers.LiveScoreSync polls only while kickoff_at is in [now-150min, now+10min]. Knockouts going to extra-time (~155min) or penalties (~170-185min) run PAST the 150-min window. Two consequences: (1) the live buzz stops updating during ET/shootout (the dramatic peak); (2) the last in-window poll wrote is_live:true, the self-reschedule chain then stops, and no finished frame (MatchStatus 0) is ever published, so is_live is left STUCK true. This is a distinct root cause from predictex-d17 (which is endpoint-failure post-match) but the same symptom; the rfm plan's deploy-note claim that it 'closes d17' is OVERSTATED — true only for matches ending <=150min after kickoff (group games do). NOT a group-stage blocker, and knockout fixtures do not yet have fifa_match_id (predictex-i9k), so this is dormant until knockout backfill. Fix options: (a) bump @post_min to ~210 (covers ET+pens+finished frame; cheap one-liner; minor extra polling of finished group games); (b) add an independent windowed is_live auto-clear sweep (robust: also self-heals a dropped/crashed final frame); recommend (b) or (a)+(b). Also correct the d17-closure scope note. Source: lib/predictex/workers/live_score_sync.ex @post_min.
close_reason: Shipped in v0.11.2 (commit 5d8fad4): @post_min 150->210 covers knockout ET/pens + the finished frame; independent clear_stuck_live/1 sweep auto-clears stuck is_live (status=:completed OR past-window). Deployed to prod — Deploy job ran (not skipped), /health 200. End-to-end behavioral validation on a real knockout (ET/shootout + is_live clear) tracked on predictex-hco for first KO 2026-06-28.

title: Auto-start unified live capture (buzz + event-source recording) from schedule
description: We missed RECORDING England v Croatia because capture (FifaLiveCapture/LiveScoreSync) must be armed by hand and stops between matches. Unify + automate: fold the spike's raw-body persistence INTO Workers.LiveScoreSync so one ~30s poll (a) saves the /detail body to fifa_captures (event source), (b) decodes -> live_* on the fixture, (c) broadcasts {:live_update}. Then auto-start from the fixture schedule via an Oban Cron entry that arms the poller ~5-10 min before each fixture.kickoff_at (fixtures with a fifa_match_id), so the buzz goes live automatically AND every match is recorded/replayable. Retire or keep FifaLiveCapture for ad-hoc. RECOMMENDED approach X (unify one worker) per brainstorm 2026-06-17; confirm X vs Y (keep two workers, auto-start both) before building. Also fixes predictex bug where is_live can stay true post-match (a windowed sweep / auto-stop falls out of the scheduler).
design:
Producer/subscriber capture model (refined 2026-06-17, user's framing). ONE worker = the PRODUCER: each ~30s tick fetches ONE /detail snapshot and publishes it on Phoenix.PubSub (e.g. topic 'capture:{match_id}'). Independent SUBSCRIBERS consume it:
 - LiveMatch: decode -> write live_* -> broadcast {:live_update} on 'fixture:{id}' (the buzz; FixtureLive already listens).
 - EventStore: persist the raw body to fifa_captures (the event source that replay reads, predictex-i1s).
 - future: match-day flourishes (predictex-3oo), analytics, a live replay-tee.
Worker knows nothing about its consumers -> add observers without touching it.

TRADE-OFF to settle at build time: Phoenix.PubSub is in-memory + at-most-once. If the EventStore subscriber is down/crashes while handling a message, that snapshot is lost -> a small gap in the recording (replay self-corrects at the next 30s snapshot). For a small friends-group game that's negligible and the symmetric subscriber model is cleanest. If lossless recording ever matters, promote persistence INTO the producer (persist synchronously BEFORE publishing) so the event source is authoritative and only reactive consumers ride PubSub — a localized change. DEFAULT: pure subscriber model; revisit if gaps appear.
notes: PLAN written: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-17-auto-capture.md (7 TDD tasks, each commit deployable). Producer/subscriber over PubSub as designed; shared Predictex.LiveScore decoder (also for i1s); Capture store promoted from Spike (table fifa_captures kept); Recorder + LiveUpdater subscribers (config-gated off in test); Oban Cron */5 auto-start + unique jobs; pre-kickoff window (kickoff-10min..+150min); retire FifaLiveCapture. Closes the manual-arm gap (missed England) and the is_live-stuck edge predictex-d17.
close_reason: Auto-start unified live capture shipped: 6-task TDD via subagent-driven-development, all on main (4d0c566..7e32c46), full gate green (335 tests). Whole-branch opus review READY-WITH-FIXES, no Critical, 5 invariants hold. Producer/PubSub-subscriber arch: shared Predictex.LiveScore decoder; Spike retired -> permanent Predictex.Capture; Capture.Recorder (event source) + Live.Updater (buzz) subscribers; LiveScoreSync producer publishes on fifa:snapshots, auto-started by Oban Cron */5 with unique:[period:40,states:[:scheduled]] (the only value excluding :executing AND warning-clean on Oban 2.23). Follow-ups filed: predictex-cvx (knockout window truncation, P2), predictex-l3n (LOWs polish, P3). NOT deployed — needs vX.Y.Z tag, user's production-readiness call.

title: Match kickoff times display 1 hour off (BST/timezone not applied)
description: Fixture kickoff times render 1 hour off for UK users — consistent with BST (UTC+1) not being applied. kickoff_at is stored as :utc_datetime (see Fixture schema); the offset suggests times are shown as raw UTC instead of being converted to Europe/London, which is currently UTC+1 (BST, runs late Mar–late Oct, so active during the Jun 11–Jul 19 tournament). Check the display/formatting layer in the LiveViews (e.g. fixture cards, my_predictions, leaderboard) for where kickoff_at is rendered without TZ conversion.
design:
PREFERRED FIX: render kickoff times in the PLAYERS BROWSER timezone, not a server-side zone.

WHY (supersedes the earlier "convert to Europe/London" idea): players may watch from anywhere. A server-side Europe/London conversion fixes UK users but is wrong for everyone else. The browser already knows each viewer's zone + locale, so let the client format. This also makes the original BST symptom a special case of the general fix.

RECOMMENDED APPROACH — pure client-side formatting (no tzdata, no round-trip):
1. Server renders the raw UTC instant in a data attribute / <time> element, e.g. <time data-utc={DateTime.to_iso8601(fixture.kickoff_at)} phx-hook="LocalTime">…</time>.
2. A Phoenix LiveView JS hook (assets/js/app.js) formats on mounted() AND updated() using Intl.DateTimeFormat with NO explicit timeZone (defaults to the browser zone) + the browser locale. DST handled automatically by the browser.
3. Provide a sensible server-rendered fallback inside the <time> (e.g. UTC label) so non-JS / pre-connect render is not blank.

TRADE-OFF (the one real decision): pure client-side means a brief flash of the server fallback (UTC) before the hook runs on the connected LiveView render. Acceptable for a display value; mitigate by rendering "<time> … UTC" as the fallback (honest, not wrong) or visually de-emphasising until formatted. AVOID the heavier alternative (push Intl timezone to the server on connect, format with tzdata) unless the time is needed server-side — it is NOT: all kickoff_at comparisons (round opens_at gating, the LiveScoreSync live-window guard) stay UTC-vs-UTC and correct. Conversion belongs ONLY at the render edge.

SCOPE: find every place kickoff_at is rendered (fixture cards, my_predictions, leaderboard, admin) and route them through the one <time>+hook helper. No migration, no tzdata dependency, no domain logic change.
notes:
SCOPE CONFIRMED 2026-06-17 (grep + read). Render surface is ONE place, not many:

RENDER SITES (all of them):
- lib/predictex_web/components/predictex_components.ex:389-390 — the ONLY formatting helper: defp kickoff/1, uses Calendar.strftime(dt, "%a %d %b · %H:%M"). This is the single point to change.
- Called once, at predictex_components.ex:153, inside the FixtureCard component header ({kickoff(@fx.fixture.kickoff_at)}).
- FixtureCard is consumed in ONE LiveView: lib/predictex_web/live/my_predictions_live.ex:89. Earlier assumption that leaderboard/dashboard/admin also render kickoff is FALSE — they do not. A repo-wide grep found NO other strftime/Calendar/date formatting anywhere in lib/predictex_web. Kickoff time is shown only on My Predictions.

NON-RENDER kickoff_at uses (leave UTC, do NOT touch): predictions.ex:128-129 locked?/2 (UTC compare), dashboard.ex:21 order_by, results/openfootball.ex + ingest.ex (ingest), fifa/crosswalk.ex (match key). All correct as UTC.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAIL — uses Phoenix 1.8 COLOCATED HOOKS: assets/js/app.js does `import {hooks as colocatedHooks} from "phoenix-colocated/predictex"` and `hooks: {...colocatedHooks}`. So the new LocalTime hook should be COLOCATED in predictex_components.ex via a <script :type={Phoenix.LiveView.ColocatedHook} name=".LocalTime"> block next to the markup — NO app.js edit required (supersedes the design-note suggestion to add a hook to app.js).

EDIT SHAPE:
1. predictex_components.ex:153 — replace {kickoff(...)} with a <time datetime={DateTime.to_iso8601(dt)} phx-hook=".LocalTime"> carrying a server fallback (e.g. the current strftime output, or "… UTC"). Keep kickoff(nil) -> "TBC".
2. Colocated LocalTime hook: format el via Intl.DateTimeFormat(undefined, {weekday:"short", day:"2-digit", month:"short", hour:"2-digit", minute:"2-digit"}) on mounted() + updated() — browser zone, matches the "%a %d %b · %H:%M" style.
No migration, no tzdata, no app.js change. One component + one colocated hook.
close_reason: Fixed via idiomatic server-side per-viewer timezone (not the initial client JS hook, which was reworked out). JS reports the browser IANA zone once via LiveSocket _tz connect param + a tz cookie; a :browser plug copies the cookie to session; PredictexWeb.TimeZone on_mount assigns @tz (connect-param when connected, session/cookie on dead render, Etc/UTC fallback) to the :require_authenticated_player live_session; pure testable PredictexWeb.TimeHelpers.kickoff/2 shifts UTC->local via the tz lib (defensive UTC fallback on bad/spoofed zones). local_time/1 renders server-localized <time>. Commit 1e0e125 (supersedes client-hook 717a663). Opus review approved; 347 tests, assets.build clean. On main locally; NOT deployed (needs tag).

title: Import (/import) has no discoverable route — players can't find how to get started
description: The /import flow (predictex-4ar, shipped v0.8.0) is live but UNLINKED: nav has Leaderboard / My Picks / Log in / Join the league, with no entry to /import. New players land on the leaderboard with no obvious 'import your picks / get started' path. Add a discoverable entry: a nav link for authenticated players and/or a prominent CTA on the My Picks page (esp. empty state). Onboarding gap.
close_reason: Added a primary 'Import picks' nav link (→/import) for authenticated players in root.html.heex — discoverable from every page. Verified in browser.

title: Leaderboard mislabels points as 'fixtures' on champion card
description: The champion spotlight on /(LeaderboardLive) renders '{fixtures_total} fixtures · {round_bonus_total} bonus' (leaderboard_live.ex:70). But fixtures_total is POINTS earned from fixture predictions (Standings: sum of result.fixture_total), not a count of fixtures — so the leader shows e.g. '250 fixtures' when 250 is a points total and there are only ~104 fixtures total. Misleading. Fix: relabel to make clear it's points from fixtures (e.g. '250 from fixtures · 0 bonus'). Check the leaderboard_row component for the same fixtures/bonus columns.
notes: Second instance of the same mislabel found & fixed: my_predictions_live.ex:56 (My Picks dashboard) also read '{n} fixtures · {n} bonus' → now 'from fixtures'. Regression assertion added to my_predictions_live_test.exs too. Both card labels now consistent.
close_reason: Champion card now reads 'N from fixtures · N bonus' (was 'N fixtures', misread as a count); row columns get title tooltips clarifying fixture vs bonus points. Regression test added; verified in browser. Fixed in leaderboard_live.ex:70 + predictex_components.ex.

title: Mum-proof FIFA import guide (esp. mobile flow)
description:
A dead-simple, friendly, step-by-step guide for importing your FIFA picks — clear enough that a non-technical 75-year-old could follow it unaided. The 'mum test' is the bar.

Current /import UX is the problem: it says 'Drag this button to your bookmarks bar' (desktop-only) and the fallback is 'run it in the browser console and paste the JSON it prints here' — pure developer jargon, and the console path is basically impossible on a phone. Most of the group will be on mobile in the WhatsApp chat, so the mobile path needs the most thought.

Goal: platform-aware, illustrated, plain-language onboarding with a 'did it work?' confirmation — no scary words ('bookmarklet', 'JSON', 'console' all banned from the user-facing copy).
design:
- Detect platform (desktop vs mobile) and show the RIGHT flow only — don't make the user choose.
- DESKTOP: numbered steps with screenshots/short gifs — (1) drag the 'Import my picks' button to your bookmarks bar, (2) open FIFA and sign in, (3) click the button, (4) we show you what we found. Rename the bookmarklet to plain language ('Import my picks'); never say 'bookmarklet'.
- MOBILE (the hard part) — candidate flows, pick/validate one:
  1. Screenshot → admin entry: user screenshots their FIFA picks page and sends it to the group admin, who already enters predictions from screenshots (existing admin flow). Zero-tech for the user; arguably the true mum-path on mobile.
  2. Copy-paste: copy the FIFA data and paste into /import — but obtaining the data on mobile (no console) is the blocker; needs a share/copy affordance from FIFA that we can't control.
  3. Guided mobile add-bookmark: iOS Safari 'add bookmark then edit the address to start with the magic text' — fiddly, fails the mum test; likely reject.
  4. Server-side fetch from a per-user FIFA share URL: user pastes a link, server fetches — gated by same-origin/auth (already hit this 'same-origin fetch gate').
- Replace the console-fallback copy entirely with mum-friendly wording regardless of flow.
- Relates to existing import work: ImportLive (/import), the colocated .FifaFragment hook, and [[predictex-i9k]] (knockout import).
acceptance_criteria:
- On desktop, a first-time user can import following only the on-screen steps, no outside help.
- On mobile, there is a clearly-signposted path that completes an import (or routes to the admin-screenshot path) without needing a console or bookmarklet drag.
- User-facing copy contains no developer jargon ('bookmarklet', 'JSON', 'console').
- A clear success/confirmation state tells the user their picks are in.
notes: CORRECTION: earlier 'DEPLOYED & VERIFIED' note was WRONG — those were plain pushes to main, which SKIP the tag-gated Deploy job (see memory deploy-requires-a-git-tag). The 4ar feature only actually reached prod with tag v0.8.0 (2026-06-17), Deploy job completed/success, verified live. The intermediate prod checks passed only because the /import route pre-existed from xox.
close_reason: Group-stage mum-proof FIFA import shipped & deployed to prod (run 27661365740, health 200). Smoke-tested in real browser; one bug (placeholder interpolation) found & fixed. Follow-ups tracked: dnp/066/fs6/xww.

title: Source live in-play fixture scores (feed spike)
description:
Spike: find and validate a source of LIVE in-play scores for World Cup 2026 fixtures. The existing Predictex.Results.Ingest / ResultSync pulls FINAL results from the openfootball feed; the live drill-down/buzz feature (predictex-c46) needs the current score WHILE a match is in progress.

Investigate: does openfootball expose in-play scores, or do we need another feed (FIFA match-predictor API already used for cohort/import? a live-scores API?)? Determine update cadence, rate limits, fixture-id crosswalk, and whether a polling Oban cron (like ResultSync) suffices. Fallback: admin-entered live score as a manual stopgap.

Output: a recommendation + the Fixture.status :live transition wiring (status enum already supports :live).
design:
LIVE IN-PLAY SCORE FEED — RECOMMENDATION (scouted 2026-06-17, tournament already live)

FINDING: openfootball CANNOT serve live scores. worldcup.json is wiki/manually updated; its own README states data is NOT live-updated. Our Results.Ingest (lib/predictex/results/ingest.ex) pulls FINAL results only every 15 min (ResultSync cron */15). So a separate live source is required.

== PHASE A: BUILD NOW (source-agnostic, the durable part) ==
New LiveScoreSync Oban worker mirroring CohortSync (lib/predictex/workers/cohort_sync.ex): injectable source_fun, Gather->Decide->Act.
- Cron: every minute, but GUARDED — short-circuit with NO API call unless >=1 fixture is in a live window (kickoff_at <= now <= kickoff_at+150min). Bounds requests to match-day windows.
- Act: update ONLY status::live + provisional home_goals/away_goals via Tournament.update_fixture/2.
- AUTHORITY RULE (critical): openfootball/ResultSync stays the SOLE authority for FINAL results + first_scorer (these feed scoring/standings). The live feed must NOT write canonical completed/scored data — only the in-play :live provisional score for the buzz UI. On feed full-time, let the next openfootball sync confirm canonical score + first-scorer. Prevents a flaky live feed from corrupting scored standings.
- Crosswalk: reuse existing Fifa.Crosswalk.match_key/3 ({utc_date, team MapSet}) — zero schema change, same approach as CohortSync. No new external-id column needed.
- Fallback: expose home_goals/away_goals/status edit in admin_fixtures_live.ex for manual live entry. Schema already supports it; low effort; degradation path if a feed dies mid-tournament.

== PHASE B: PICK THE FEED (deferred decision; validate against a real live match) ==
Opt 1 FIFA-native undocumented (PROBE FIRST, zero cost): app already integrates play.fifa.com CDN + FIFA crosswalk. FIFA site is driven by undocumented api.fifa.com/api/v3 (historically a liveNow filter, matchId-keyed). If it works for 2026 it is the winner: no new dependency, reuse crosswalk, free. RISK: undocumented/unstable. Probe-able NOW (matches live since Jun 11).
Opt 2 football-data.org EUR12/mo livescores (RECOMMENDED paid primary): reputable, stable, 10 req/min (ample w/ windowed polling — one call returns all live matches), clean fixtures+team names -> existing crosswalk. ~EUR12-24 total, cancel after Jul 19. Best reliability-per-euro.
Opt 3 BALLDONTLIE FIFA (fifa.balldontlie.io): nicest in-play fields (clock_display 47:15, status, match_number maps cleanly to openfootball). Live needs paid ($9.99 ALL-STAR verify matches incl., $39.99 GOAT all). Free tier 5 req/min is teams/stadiums only.
Opt 4 worldcup26.ir (rezarahiminia free OSS): free, no key, 500 req/min, claims real-time but UNPROVEN, single-maintainer, no SLA. OK as best-effort ONLY paired with admin manual fallback, not sole source.
AVOID: API-Football free (100 req/day too low for live polling); Sportmonks (no permanent free tier).

RECOMMENDATION: Build Phase A against the abstraction now. Probe FIFA-native endpoint against a real live match (possible today); if it does not pan out, use football-data.org EUR12/mo as reliable primary. Admin manual entry is the always-available fallback. Provider choice is a swap-one-module change thanks to source_fun injection.
notes:
FIFA-NATIVE PROBE RESULTS (2026-06-17 13:11Z) — THIS IS THE RECOMMENDED SOURCE, supersedes the paid-feed options.

api.fifa.com/api/v3 is LIVE, FREE, NO API KEY. Two endpoints we need:

A) LIVE POLL (lightweight): GET https://api.fifa.com/api/v3/live/football/now
   -> {Results:[...]} = only matches currently in play across ALL FIFA comps; empty [] when none (verified empty at 13:11Z, no WC match in play). Filter Results by IdCompetition=="17". This is FIFAs own match-centre live feed -> designed to tick in real time. IDEAL for the windowed LiveScoreSync poll.

B) FULL WC CALENDAR: GET https://api.fifa.com/api/v3/calendar/matches?idCompetition=17&idSeason=285023&count=200&language=en
   -> all 104 WC2026 matches. Per match: IdMatch, HomeTeamScore/AwayTeamScore, MatchStatus, MatchTime ("97\x27" minute string), Period, Winner, HomeTeamPenaltyScore/AwayTeamPenaltyScore, Date (UTC Z), Home/Away.TeamName.

MatchStatus enum decoded from live data: 0 = FINISHED (has scores + MatchTime), 1 = UPCOMING (scores null). LIVE is a 3rd code (not yet observed; classic FIFA value is 3) + Period/MatchTime populated. <-- ONLY remaining unknown.

CROSSWALK IS TRIVIAL: v3 IdMatch (e.g. 400021496) == the fifaId field ALREADY in the rounds.json our app pulls (Fifa.Reference: play.fifa.com/json/match_predictor/rounds.json carries fifaId + homeScore/awayScore + matchcentreUrl /match/17/285023/289273/400021443). So: 17=idCompetition, 285023=idSeason, 289273=idStage. Either (a) match v3 IdMatch to rounds.json fifaId, or (b) add a one-time fifa_id column to Fixture sourced from rounds.json, or (c) reuse existing Fifa.Crosswalk.match_key (Date+TeamName). Zero fragile guessing.

IMPACT ON RECOMMENDATION: FIFA-native wins — free, no key, reuses Req + FIFA crosswalk already in the app, gives live minute (MatchTime) for the buzz UI. football-data.org EUR12/mo demoted to fallback-B (only if FIFA proves flaky mid-tournament). Admin manual entry stays as always-available stopgap. Authority rule UNCHANGED: openfootball/ResultSync remains sole authority for FINAL score + first_scorer (scoring); FIFA live feed only sets status :live + provisional in-play score.

REMAINING TO CONFIRM (needs an in-play match; next = Portugal v Congo DR 17:00Z today): exact live MatchStatus code + that live/football/now scores update in real time. Low risk (it powers fifa.com live). After that, the spike is fully closed and ready to build LiveScoreSync against endpoint A.
close_reason: Spike complete: FIFA v3 live contract decoded (live MatchStatus=3 confirmed from the Portugal v DR Congo capture, 696 rows), capture worker shipped. Its persistence folds into predictex-rfm (FifaLiveCapture retired there). c46 shipped on top.

title: Live fixture drill-down + what-if leaderboard buzz
description:
When a fixture is LIVE, let a member expand/drill into it to see the social 'what-if' picture in real time — the core group-chat buzz loop from the design brief ('Predict the scores. Win the group chat.').

User-facing behaviour:
- Expand/drill into a live fixture (in-place expand on a 'Live now' section of the leaderboard, or a /fixtures/:id view) to see EVERYONE's locked predictions for that fixture (scoreline, booster, and in knockouts first-team/first-scorer).
- Show how the CURRENT live score would change the leaderboard — a provisional/projected standings computed as if the fixture finished at the current score (not persisted).
- Generate 'buzz' scenarios: e.g. 'if Brazil scores, Sav moves to #1', 'if it ends 2–1, you overtake Dave'. Surface the rank deltas for a few plausible next outcomes (home scores → h+1, away scores → a+1, full-time-at-current).

Why: this is the headline social feature — it turns a live match into shared drama in the group chat. High engagement value during match days.
design:
- Prediction visibility: only reveal others' picks AFTER kickoff (gate on Dashboard/Predictions.locked?). Showing picks before kickoff would let players copy — hard rule.
- Projected scoring: add a pure 'projection' mode to Predictex.Standings/Scoring — e.g. Standings.project(fixture_id, home, away) returning a projected leaderboard WITHOUT persisting. Must honour booster (2x) and risky/cohort bonus for the projection to be accurate (or document the simplification).
- Scenario set: compute leaderboard for {end-at-current, home+1, away+1} and diff vs current standings to extract rank-change narratives.
- Real-time: ideally re-project via PubSub as the live score ticks — shares infra with [[predictex-3oo]] (event-triggered flourishes / PubSub on results sync).
- Reuses standings compute heavily → relates to [[predictex-a4j]] (cache/scope Standings.leaderboard/0). Needs current_scope to phrase 'you overtake X' → relates to [[predictex-kzz]] ('YOU' highlight on public leaderboard).
- Placement TBD: 'Live now' card on the leaderboard front door vs dedicated drill-down route.
acceptance_criteria:
- A live fixture is drillable; non-live fixtures are not.
- Drill-down lists all players' picks for that fixture, only once the fixture has kicked off.
- A projected leaderboard reflects the current live score (booster + risky bonus honoured), computed without persisting.
- At least the 'if it ends now' and 'if home/away scores next' scenarios produce correct rank-change statements.
- 'YOU' framing works for the logged-in viewer.
notes: REDESIGN SHIPPED v0.10.2: FixtureLive rebuilt on the design system — projected what-if standings with ▲/▼ movement arrows, ⚡ buzz headlines ('X moves up to #N'), team flags, font-score, LIVE pulse, 'you' highlight. Buzz engine extended (scenarios_with_deltas/3, headlines/4). Demo data: Predictex.Demo (tagged @demo.predictex.local, purge/0 removes) — FIXED scoreline variety (was all 1-0 -> no movement; now palette generator so 6 players diverge per fixture -> real overtakes). Verified live on prod fixture 61 (Portugal v DR Congo): headlines fire, ranks shuffle. 333 tests. NOTE: built on the design SYSTEM, not the Claude Design mockup (link is auth-gated/403 — file never obtained). OPEN for England v Croatia 20:00Z: poller must be re-armed after kickoff (rpc LiveScoreSync.start()) since it stops between match windows — demo players already have varied predictions on that fixture.
close_reason: Live fixture drill-down + what-if buzz shipped v0.10.2, live on prod. All AC met for group stage: live-gated drillability (UI entry points gate on is_live), post-kickoff anti-copy picks reveal, projected leaderboard honouring booster+risky (Standings.project/3 reuses real rank/2 path), end-now/home-next/away-next scenarios with tested rank-change statements (buzz_test.exs AC#4), and YOU framing. 11 tests pass. Knockout first-team/first-scorer picks display deferred to predictex-hco (can't exercise until first KO 2026-06-28; needs hco backfill data).

title: Auto-sync FIFA cohort % (risky-bonus data) from matchStats.json
description: Spike (docs/superpowers/research/2026-06-16-xox-fifa-import-spike.md) found FIFA publishes per-outcome cohort percentages at play.fifa.com/json/match_predictor/matchStats.json, keyed by matchId, server-fetchable (HTTP 200 plain curl, NOT Akamai-gated). homeWin/draw/awayWin map exactly to cohort_home_pct/cohort_draw_pct/cohort_away_pct. Today these are admin-entered (a02) and the risky bonus is silently skipped when unset. Build a plain Oban worker (mt6 substrate): Req.get matchStats.json + rounds.json, map matchId -> Fixture (crosswalk by kickoff date/teams), upsert cohort_*_pct. No bookmarklet, no member action. Also exposes firstGoalIsOwn + results as a cross-check. Separate from xox (public stats, not member predictions).
close_reason: Shipped in v0.5.0. mt6: hourly... ResultSync Oban worker (every 15m) runs Ingest.sync_from_url. 7ux: CohortSync Oban worker (hourly) pulls FIFA matchStats.json, pure Fifa.Cohort.plan joins by {utc_date,team-set} + orientation, overwrites cohort_*_pct.

title: e5o v2: fill both-placeholder R32 fixtures FIFA has resolved (projection-validated orientation)
description: e5o v1 is anchored-only: it fills a placeholder side ONLY when the other side is already a resolved real team (the anchor fixes orientation + validates the slot match). Skip if no projectable side / projection matches neither FIFA name (no blind positional fill — the advisor rejected that in v1). Reuses GroupTables + Crosswalk.norm + the e5o fill machinery + the ahi no-downgrade guard. Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-27-e5o-both-placeholder-resolution-design.md

title: Dashboard live updates via fifa:snapshots PubSub (drop 30s poll)
description:
Follow-up to predictex-doz. The dashboard live-tick currently polls (re-runs Dashboard.for_player ~7 queries, incl. Standings.leaderboard/0) every 30s per connected tab while any match is live. Live-score changes are already a data event on the fifa:snapshots PubSub topic (FixtureLive subscribes). If MyPredictionsLive subscribed too, the timer's only job would be the two clock thresholds (−30min preview, kickoff lock) and the 30s score-poll branch in next_tick_delay/2 could be removed — instant live updates, zero score-polling.

## Context
Origin: final whole-branch review of predictex-doz flagged Standings.leaderboard()-per-tick as a scaling lever (trivial at ~7 players, informational). Deferred option documented in docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-19-dashboard-live-tick-design.md (Deferred option section). Blocked-by/relates: predictex-doz.
design: Subscribe MyPredictionsLive to the relevant fixture/snapshot topic(s) on connected mount (dashboard spans many fixtures → single aggregate topic or per-fixture subs for the in-play set). Add handle_info for the snapshot/live-update msg that re-pulls (or patches) dash. Drop the @live_poll_ms (30_000) branch from Dashboard.fixture_delay/2 so the timer only sleeps to the next −30min/kickoff threshold. Confirm next_tick_delay/2 still schedules correctly when the only pending work is clock thresholds (no live fixture keeping it awake).
acceptance_criteria: Live score on /predictions updates from a fifa:snapshots broadcast without the 30s poll; next_tick_delay/2 no longer returns 30_000 for in-play fixtures (the live branch removed); domain tests updated; gate green.
notes: CODE-COMPLETE + REVIEWED, committed LOCAL (3cde06d), pending deploy. TDD (5 units): Tournament.subscribe_changes/broadcast_change (one coarse 'fixtures:changed' topic) ← broadcast post-DB-write by LiveScore.apply_to_fixture/2 (live, +its per-fixture feed) AND Ingest.commit/1 (settle, once/run); MyPredictionsLive subscribes once + re-pulls on :fixtures_changed; Dashboard.next_tick_delay/2 dropped the @live_poll_ms(30s) branch (nil past kickoff). Advisor design call: settle-broadcast from ResultSync makes the dashboard FASTER than the old poll (DB only learns settle at sync time anyway) — not scope creep, it's 9p0's own thesis (the one data-change that wasn't yet a PubSub event). Scope guard: FixtureLive NOT migrated (its latent settle gap unchanged). Independent code review: Ready to deploy, no Critical/Important; applied its Important (assert_receive→assert_received, window-free on the global topic) + Minor (clear_live coarse-broadcast test). Gate 404 green. Acceptance met. Deferred polish (not blocking): minute-only live changes still trigger a full re-pull (changed? gate; same cost as old poll).
close_reason: Shipped in v0.11.10 (deployed 2026-06-20: Deploy success, /health 200, anon / 200). /predictions live updates via the coarse Tournament 'fixtures:changed' PubSub topic (broadcast post-DB-write by LiveScore.apply_to_fixture/2 + Ingest.commit/1); 30s poll removed (Dashboard.next_tick_delay/2 drops the live branch). TDD 5 units + opus review clean. Deferred follow-up noted on issue: minute-only live change still triggers a full re-pull.

title: Test-suite async-safety review: get ConnCase live tests back to async
description: The original predictex-uhf async goal is unfinished. When live_buzz was force-flipped to async:true, the full suite showed intermittent cross-module failures in FixtureLiveTest round!/0 (Tournament.create_round) — which never touches FunWithFlags, so the cause is concurrency/isolation, not flags. After contracting live_buzz, fixture_live_test/leaderboard_live_test/my_predictions_live_test no longer mutate any global state and were LEFT at async:false (stale-but-safe) pending this review. Investigate the round!/0 cross-module race (likely a sandbox-ownership or unique-constraint interaction under higher max_cases), then flip those files to async:true and verify green across multiple seeds (mix test --seed 0/1/2/42/7). Acceptance: the 3 files run async:true with the full suite green across >=5 seeds.
close_reason: Got the 3 ConnCase live-test files (fixture_live, leaderboard, my_predictions) to async:true. Root cause was TWO independent issues, not the bead's single 'round!/0 unique-constraint' guess: (1) Replay.Cache — a supervised singleton GenServer (test-gated-out of the app tree, started via start_supervised! in the replay describe) reads the Repo in its OWN process, so under async/isolated sandbox it had no ownership → DBConnection.OwnershipError on the 4 replay tests. Fixed with Ecto.Adapters.SQL.Sandbox.allow/3 granting it the test's connection (race-free: module tests are sequential, only this block uses the Cache). (2) rounds.ordinal unique-index DEADLOCK (40P01): live_ids_test 'assign/1' inserted KO ordinal 4 before group ordinal 1 (descending) while every other async module inserts ascending → concurrent sandbox transactions formed a lock cycle, PG killed one. This is the real 'known intermittent flake'/Postgrex-disconnect; MAIN was 0/8 but the async flip raised it to ~25% on seed 0. Fixed by reordering to ascending; the suite-wide 'create rounds ascending' invariant is now documented in DataCase.setup_sandbox (where a test author will hit it), so a 40P01 recurrence is self-diagnosing. The bead's 'unique-constraint' intuition was half-right (it IS a unique-index interaction in create_round) but missed the Replay.Cache cause. Verified: full suite 0/20 failures (heavy on seed 0); mix precommit green (499 passed). NOTE: the 'Postgrex disconnected' log line is known-benign sandbox-owner teardown noise — present on green main, never correlates with a failure; not the flake.

title: Match live page: 'if your pick lands' projected leaderboard
description:
On the match live/fixture page, add a what-if view: 'if YOUR predicted result comes in exactly, what would the leaderboard look like?' — i.e. project the standings assuming this member's own scoreline prediction for this fixture is correct.

Distinct from the existing live-buzz what-if (predictex-c46), which recomputes the leaderboard from the CURRENT LIVE score. This one is hypothetical on the MEMBER'S PICK landing, viewable pre-kickoff and during play, before the result is settled — it answers 'where do I end up if I'm right here?'

Scope notes / open questions:
- Whose pick: the viewing member's own prediction for this fixture (per-user projection). Consider also showing it for the row you're looking at in 'Everyone's Picks'.
- Score the hypothetical via the SAME scoring engine the real leaderboard uses (do not reimplement points logic) — feed it the predicted scoreline as if it were the final result, recompute standings, diff vs current.
- Surface the rank delta (e.g. 'you'd move 4th -> 2nd') consistent with the existing buzz/rank-delta styling.
- Reuse the c46 what-if leaderboard recompute path if it can be parameterised by an arbitrary assumed result rather than the live score.
- Likely flag-gated like other live-buzz UI until verified.

Acceptance (draft):
- Live/fixture page shows a projected leaderboard assuming the member's pick for this fixture is the final result.
- Projection uses the real scoring engine (no duplicated points math).
- Shows the member's rank change vs the current standings.
notes: Code-complete + reviewed (feature-dev:code-reviewer: no material issues) + gate green (433 tests), committed local 747d6f1 (UNPUSHED). Surface: Predictions.get_player_fixture_prediction/2; Buzz.pick_projection/4 + enrich_rows/2; FixtureLive @pick_projection + #pick-projection section. Anti-copy resolved via render gate (pre-kickoff: viewer row only; post-kickoff: full board on @picks_visible?). v1 scoreline-only (knockout caveat). Spec/plan in docs/superpowers/. REMAINING before close: real-browser eyeball of the card pre-kickoff (own row, no leak) and post-kickoff (full board). Awaiting user push/deploy call.
close_reason: Eyeballed in prod (v0.11.12): 'If your pick lands' verified pre-kickoff (own row + 'you'd be #N' headline, no other names) and during live play (full board, viewer row highlighted). Working as designed.

title: Add a Content-Security-Policy header (sobelow Config.CSP, high confidence)
description: sobelow (added in predictex-kvo) flags Config.CSP: Missing Content-Security-Policy (High Confidence) on the :browser pipeline in lib/predictex_web/router.ex:12. Accepted into .sobelow-skips for now (not silently buried) — adding a CSP is real hardening but must be crafted carefully to avoid breaking: the inline theme-toggle <script> in the layout, colocated LiveView hooks, Google Fonts (style-src/font-src), and daisyUI/Tailwind inline styles. Needs browser verification. Plan: add a put_secure_browser_headers/CSP plug (or set content_security_policy) in the browser pipeline, allowing self + fonts.googleapis.com/fonts.gstatic.com + needed inline hashes/nonces; verify the app renders and LiveView connects; then remove the .sobelow-skips entry for Config.CSP.
acceptance_criteria: A CSP header is served on browser responses; app renders and LiveView connects with no console CSP violations; the Config.CSP entry is removed from .sobelow-skips and sobelow stays green.
notes: Shipped hash-based CSP (advisor steered hash over nonce: single static inline script → byte-stable → 'sha256-...' is proportionate, one-line, lower-surface, and is sobelow's expected put_secure_browser_headers shape). router.ex: @content_security_policy (default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'sha256-<theme script>'; style-src 'self' fonts.googleapis.com; font-src 'self' fonts.gstatic.com; img-src 'self' data:; connect-src 'self'; base-uri/frame-ancestors 'self'; object-src 'none') passed via put_secure_browser_headers map. Strict script-src (no unsafe-inline); strict style-src held (no inline styles in markup; topbar uses CSSOM). Non-circular guard test (test/predictex_web/csp_test.exs) recomputes the rendered inline-script sha256 and asserts it's in the header → fails if the script is edited without updating @theme_script_hash. Browser-verified via chrome-devtools on /, /players/log-in, /predictions (authed): app renders, LiveView websocket connects, inline theme script executes+toggles, Google Fonts load, ZERO CSP violations. .sobelow-skips Config.CSP entry removed; sobelow --skip --exit Low green (exit 0).
close_reason: Strict hash-based CSP shipped on the :browser pipeline and browser-verified. All AC met: (1) CSP header served via put_secure_browser_headers; (2) app renders + LiveView websocket connects + inline theme script executes/toggles + Google Fonts load with ZERO console CSP violations across /, /players/log-in, and authed /predictions (chrome-devtools); (3) Config.CSP removed from .sobelow-skips, sobelow --skip --exit Low green. Non-circular guard test added (370 tests pass, gate green).

title: Match recap: points-per-player + match breakdown (goals by type, cards)
description:
Revisit the match recap / fixture view (the per-fixture 'Everyone's Picks' page, e.g. Czech Republic v South Africa) to enrich it with a proper recap once a match is settled.

Two things to add:
1. **Points gained per player** — for the settled match, show how many leaderboard points each member earned from their pick on THIS fixture (not just their scoreline guess). Today the fixture page lists each member's predicted scoreline (davewil 2-0, Sav 2-2, ...) but does not attribute the points that pick yielded. Surface the per-fixture points contribution alongside each pick.
2. **Match breakdown** — show the actual match events: who scored and HOW (penalty / own-goal / open-play/regular goal), plus cards (yellow/red) per player. We already capture goal events with Type enum (1=penalty, 2=open play, 3=own goal) and scorer names via the FIFA v3 live detail feed (see memory fifa-v3-live-api-contract). Cards may need an additional source/field — scope what's available before committing.

Acceptance (draft):
- Settled fixture page shows each member's points earned from that fixture's pick.
- Match breakdown section lists goals with scorer name + type (pen/OG/regular) attributed to the correct scoring side (own-goal rule: side = team whose Goals array it's in).
- Cards shown if a source exists; otherwise note the gap and defer cards to a follow-up.

Notes: relates to the replay/recap work (predictex-i1s). Data source for events = FIFA v3 detail; scoring authority remains openfootball.
notes:
SCOPE UPDATE 2026-06-18: cards split out to predictex-bdq (deferred until a red/second-yellow is captured and the Card enum is verified from data). title: Countdown-to-kickoff timer on the next match (/predictions)
description: User request 2026-06-18: the NEXT upcoming match (soonest fixture with kickoff in the future) on /predictions should show a live-updating countdown to kickoff (e.g. 'Kicks off in 23:14'). Likely a client-side JS hook ticking each second (cf. the existing LocalTime hook in assets/js/app.js which already parses a datetime attr + Intl-formats) so it counts down without server round-trips; server renders kickoff_at, the hook computes remaining. Define 'next match': the single earliest fixture with kickoff_at > now (and not completed). Relates to predictex-4zu (30-min CTA window) and predictex-afm (live CTA) — when the countdown hits 0 / enters the 30-min window the card already gains the 'Match preview' CTA. Decisions still open: show on just the one next match or all upcoming within some range; format (mm:ss vs 'in 2h 14m'); behaviour at 0 (switch to 'Starting…' / live).
acceptance_criteria: The next upcoming fixture on /predictions shows a kickoff countdown that updates without a full page reload; counts down to zero and transitions sensibly; works in the viewer's timezone; covered by a test (hook contract or LiveView render of the countdown element).
notes: IMPLEMENTED on main (NOT deployed). TDD: (1) pure Dashboard.next_match/2 — soonest fixture with future kickoff, non-completed, across all rounds, else nil; 3 unit tests. (2) MyPredictionsLive renders a 'Next match' banner (teams + flags) above the rank hero with a <time phx-hook='.Countdown' data-kickoff=ISO8601> element; 2 LiveView render tests (banner present for soonest upcoming; absent when nothing upcoming). (3) Colocated .Countdown hook ticks each second, adaptive format: 'in 2d 4h' / 'in 3h 12m' / 'in mm:ss' under 1h, 'now' at zero (clears interval). Full suite 367 green; mix precommit clean. CAVEAT: JS tick is contract-verified (rendered element asserted) but NOT executed in tests (no JS runtime in LiveViewTest) — not yet eyeballed in a browser. DECISION OPEN: banner is NOT behind a feature flag (unlike live_buzz). Per docs/engineering-principles.md §2 new user-facing features should be flag-gated; this is additive/low-risk and only shows when a real upcoming fixture exists. Reused :live_buzz would violate one-flag-per-feature; a new flag is scope not requested — surfaced to user.
close_reason: Shipped v0.11.4 and verified live on prod. title: Extend /predictions live CTA to open 30 min before kickoff (+ post-match recap)
description: Follow-up to predictex-afm. Today the FixtureCard CTA to /fixtures/:id (FixtureLive) shows ONLY when fixture.is_live (predictex_components.ex:194-201, gated '@live_buzz? and @fx.fixture.is_live and @live_path'). Extend it so the link is available from 30 minutes before kickoff, stays through the live match, AND remains afterward as a post-match recap (user decisions 2026-06-18). Visibility rule becomes a single open-ended window: now >= kickoff - 30min (kept flag-gated on :live_buzz). Label varies by state: pre-kickoff (not live, not completed) -> 'Match preview ›'; live -> existing 'LIVE {minute} · {score} ›'; completed -> 'Match recap ›'. CONSEQUENCE: the window has no upper bound, so every completed fixture also gets the recap CTA. DESIGN (honour Gather->Decide->Act): add a PURE testable predicate (e.g. Predictions.cta_window?(fixture, now), sibling to locked?/2) — NO inline DateTime.utc_now() in the stateless component; MyPredictionsLive gathers 'now' and passes a precomputed live_cta? boolean attr to FixtureCard (same pattern as live_buzz?/live_path). Label selection is a pure private helper in the component over @fx.fixture. KNOWN LIMITATION to note (not in scope): FixtureLive's header shows the live score only when is_live, so a completed-match recap shows teams+kickoff+everyone's picks but not the final score prominently — possible follow-up.
acceptance_criteria: Pure cta_window?/2 unit-tested (false >30min pre; true at boundary kickoff-30min; true live; true completed; false when kickoff nil). MyPredictionsLive: CTA link to /fixtures/:id present within 30-min pre-window with text 'Match preview', present+LIVE text when live, present 'Match recap' when completed, ABSENT >30min before kickoff, ABSENT when :live_buzz off. Existing afm tests still green. mix precommit clean.
notes: IMPLEMENTED on main (NOT deployed — awaiting deploy call). TDD: (1) pure Predictions.cta_window?/2 (now >= kickoff-30min, open-ended; nil kickoff -> false) with 6 unit tests; (2) MyPredictionsLive gathers 'now' at mount and passes live_cta? boolean to FixtureCard (component stays a dumb renderer); (3) FixtureCard gate changed from is_live to live_cta?, label via pure cta_label/1 (live score / 'Match recap' completed / 'Match preview' pre-kickoff) + cta_color/1. 5 LiveView tests (preview within window, recap when completed, no-CTA >30min, no-CTA flag-off, existing live link still green). Full suite 362 passed; mix precommit clean. Decision recorded: post-match recap CTA shows on ALL completed fixtures (open-ended window) per user choice. KNOWN: FixtureLive header shows final score only when is_live, so the completed-match recap shows teams+kickoff+everyone's picks but not the final score prominently — possible follow-up.
close_reason: Shipped v0.11.3 and verified live on prod. /predictions live CTA now opens 30 min before kickoff ('Match preview'), through live (score), and after full-time ('Match recap'); pure Predictions.cta_window?/2 + live_cta? attr; 362 tests green. Deploy job RAN (build+push image, SSH recreate); prod rpc confirms cta_window?(kickoff+20m)=true / (+60m)=false, root HTTP 200; :live_buzz already enabled globally so visible to all members.

title: Live.Updater rescue: let it crash, or justify + test the rescue
description: Code-smell / OTP-resilience review (docs/engineering-principles.md §4 'rescue blocks to silence errors') found Predictex.Live.Updater.handle_info/2 (lib/predictex/live/updater.ex:24-31) wraps decode+write in a bare rescue that logs and continues, with no justifying comment and no test for the error path. VERIFIED: Updater subscribes in init/1 (line 15), so a supervisor restart re-subscribes, the poison snapshot is already out of the mailbox, and the next snapshot (~30s, LiveScoreSync chain) re-drives state. Therefore let-it-crash is the MORE idiomatic choice (a supervised crash is visible via telemetry; a Logger.error can be missed). If poison-message resilience without a restart blip is genuinely wanted, keep the rescue but ADD a comment explaining why + a test asserting the error path logs and does not broadcast.
acceptance_criteria: Either the rescue is removed (let it crash) with the subscriber relying on supervision; OR it is kept with a justifying comment AND a test covering the error path (e.g. snapshot for a missing fixture logs, no {:live_update} broadcast). Decision recorded.

CLAUDE.md documents it.

title: Centralize FunWithFlags reset in test case templates
description: Principles review (docs/engineering-principles.md §3 Test Isolation) found flag-state cleanup is scattered: DataCase/ConnCase set up only the Ecto SQL sandbox; neither resets FunWithFlags state. 5 test files (feature_flags_dashboard_test, my_predictions_live_test, fixture_live_test, leaderboard_live_test, flags_test) each self-manage with async: false + per-file on_exit(disable) — the exact 'centralize cleanup in the case template, not scattered' anti-pattern §3 names. Suite passes today, so it is FRAGILE/non-scalable, not broken. NOTE: the fun_with_flags_toggles table means flag state lives in BOTH the sandboxed DB AND an un-sandboxed ETS cache — the central reset must CLEAR THE CACHE, not rely on sandbox rollback.
acceptance_criteria: DataCase/ConnCase centrally reset FunWithFlags (DB + ETS cache) before+after each test (or via a tag); per-file async: false + on_exit flag cleanup removed where flag state was the only reason; flag tests can run async again.
close_reason: Resolved by contracting the live_buzz flag rather than centralizing its cleanup. Per the parallel-change model (flag = expand→migrate→contract) and the PO's call that live_buzz is accepted in prod, the flag was removed: gates stripped from FixtureLive/LeaderboardLive/MyPredictionsLive + fixture_card (feature now unconditional), off-path tests deleted, flag enable/disable removed from all tests, flags_test.exs deleted. With no flag, there is no flag state for DataCase/ConnCase to reset — the AC's intent (no scattered cleanup, tests don't fuss over flag state) is met by removal. FunWithFlags dep + /admin/feature-flags dashboard retained as the dark-ship mechanism for future flags. Full suite 367 green across seeds 0,1,2,42,7 + mix precommit green. NOTE: the original async goal is deferred to predictex- (filed) — flag tests stay async:false because the earlier async attempt surfaced unrelated cross-module round!/0 races. Kill-switch removed: live-feed failure mitigation is now revert+redeploy. Needs a deploy (untagged) to land in prod.

title: Add scripts/pre-deploy (local production-surface verification)
description: Principles review (docs/engineering-principles.md §1 Pre-Deploy Verification) found no scripts/pre-deploy. The doc prescribes a local pre-deploy that runs tests + Docker image build + release boot + infra-config validation BEFORE tagging. Those checks currently live only in CI's tag-triggered Deploy job (which does run a release-boot check before recreating the prod container — so the prod safety net exists remotely). This is therefore a LOCAL-FEEDBACK gap (catch a broken Docker build / boot before burning a tag+CI cycle), not a prod-safety hole.
acceptance_criteria: scripts/pre-deploy runs: mix test, format/static-analysis check, docker build, release boot eval, docker-compose.prod.yml config validation; documented in CLAUDE.md; ideally invoked by the deploy/tag workflow.
notes: Implemented scripts/pre-deploy: mix precommit + sobelow + docker build + bin/predictex eval boot smoke test (dummy SECRET_KEY_BASE/DATABASE_URL/LEAGUE_INVITE_CODE; eval does NOT start the endpoint or hit the DB). docker-compose.prod.yml is server-side (/root/predictex), so compose-config validation is out of local scope (noted in the script + CLAUDE.md). VERIFICATION STATUS: the gate (mix precommit, incl. credo) and sobelow steps PASS; the script correctly invoked docker build and surfaced its failure. But the build FAILED IN THIS SANDBOX at 'mix assets.setup' — the Tailwind binary download (GitHub releases host) has no egress here, whereas hex.pm (mix deps.compile) succeeded moments earlier. The identical Dockerfile builds in CI and shipped v0.11.4 today, so this is an environment egress limit, NOT a script/Dockerfile defect. The docker-build + boot path therefore needs ONE run on a networked machine (the user's Mac/OrbStack) to confirm end-to-end before closing.
close_reason: Verified end-to-end on networked Mac/OrbStack (Docker 29.4.0): scripts/pre-deploy ran green through mix precommit (367 tests), sobelow, docker image build (mix assets.setup Tailwind v4.3.0 + esbuild downloaded successfully — the step that failed under the egress-blocked sandbox), and the bin/predictex eval release-boot smoke test. Reached '== pre-deploy OK — safe to tag ==' (exit 0). AC met.

title: Match replay engine — replay any recorded match (stream-based) onto a demo fixture
description: SPEC: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-17-match-replay-demo-design.md. Replay the event-sourced fifa_captures of ANY recorded match as a lazy Stream (Stream.cycle for loop + Stream.zip(Stream.interval(interval_ms)) for pacing; default 1s ~= 30x speed), consumed by a stoppable host task, applied via a shared Predictex.LiveScore decoder (extracted from LiveScoreSync) to an ISOLATED demo fixture (the ONLY legal target; never a real fixture). Drives the existing /fixtures/:id buzz UI via PubSub so all viewers see it. rpc-first: Replay.start/stop. Generalized from demo-only to any match_id (no hardcoded default once unified capture lands). Needs: shared LiveScore decoder + Predictex.Demo extended with a demo round+fixture (past kickoff so picks reveal). Replay of the existing 400021502 capture works today; recording NEW matches depends on the unified-capture issue.
notes: Eyeballed in prod 2026-06-21: replay works but played too slowly (flat 1s/frame). Fixed: adaptive pacing via pure Replay.tick_delay_ms/1 (1400ms dwell on score-change frames so buzz is readable, 250ms rush through minute-only filler). Committed local 51df240 (UNPUSHED). Prod runs old pace until deployed. REMAINING before close: deploy, then re-eyeball one real replay (400021510 / 400021504).
close_reason: Replay engine shipped + adaptive pacing (v0.11.12) accepted in prod 2026-06-22. Eyeballed pre-pacing-fix (worked, too slow); pacing fix deployed and signed off. Done.

title: LiveScoreSync: is_live can stay true if FIFA detail endpoint stops returning 200 post-match
description: LiveScoreSync.apply_update only runs on {:ok, 200, body}; any other response hits the log-and-skip branch, leaving is_live frozen true until the fixture exits the 150-min window (after which it's never revisited). Per the fifa-v3-live-api-contract a finished match returns MatchStatus 0 with scores at a 200, which clears is_live correctly — so this only bites if FIFA 404s/500s a just-finished match before a clean 0 is observed. The original spec's /now-membership design would have treated 'match disappeared' as the finish signal. Options: (a) a stale-window sweep that clears is_live for fixtures past kickoff+window, (b) fall back to /now membership for finish detection. Watch during first live enablement (Portugal v Congo DR was the first scheduled live test). Surfaced by the predictex-c46 whole-branch review.
notes: Root cause (endpoint stops returning 200 post-match → is_live stuck true) is now self-healed by predictex-cvx's clear_stuck_live/1 sweep in Workers.LiveScoreSync: the status-branch clears is_live within ~one ResultSync cycle (≤15min) once openfootball marks status=:completed, even while the fixture is still in the (DB-query) capture window and the chain is alive — exactly the d17 scenario. Covered by the 'd17 fast-path' unit test (completed fixture, chain running → is_live cleared). Close together with cvx after the same prod validation; keeping open as the explicit d17 acceptance check.
close_reason: Self-healed by predictex-cvx's clear_stuck_live/1 status-branch: once openfootball marks status=:completed, the sweep clears is_live within ~one ResultSync cycle even while the producer chain is alive and regardless of FIFA endpoint health — exactly the d17 endpoint-stall root cause. Shipped + deployed in v0.11.2; covered by the d17 fast-path unit test. Prod validation shared with hco.

title: Pivot: run the game natively in-app (members predict here); keep only FIFA risky/cohort import
description:
STRATEGIC PIVOT (deferred — 'for later', not yet committed). Today members make their picks ON FIFA and pull them into Predictex via the self-import flow (predictex-xox/4ar: bookmarklet + mobile copy-paste). Proposal: members enter predictions DIRECTLY in this app (native scoreline + booster per fixture), removing the FIFA round-trip for picks entirely.

KEEP importing FIFA's 'risky' data: the public cohort / popular-pick stats (matchStats.json homeWin/draw/awayWin %, quickPicks) that drive the risky bonus. That's already server-fetchable and handled by the CohortSync Oban worker — no member action, no auth gate — so it survives the pivot untouched.

NET: the only remaining FIFA dependency becomes the public risky/cohort feed; member predictions become first-class in Predictex.
design:
Native prediction entry for members:
- Per-fixture scoreline entry (home/away goals) + one booster per round, mirroring the existing Prediction contract and admin entry (predictex-a02) but self-service for the logged-in player.
- Reuse existing scoring (Scoring/Standings) and kickoff lockout — picks lock at kickoff; pre-kickoff editable. Validate at the boundary (anti-corruption layer) per LiveView discipline.
- Likely a new member-facing edit LiveView (or extend My Picks /predictions from read-only to editable), replacing the 'Edit on FIFA' links.

Keep: CohortSync worker + matchStats.json risky/cohort import (drives risky bonus).

Deprecate if this lands (supersedes): the member self-import surface — /import flow, Fifa.Import.plan + rows_from_envelope, bookmarklet, ImportLive — and its open follow-ups predictex-066 (iOS Shortcut), predictex-dnp (import screenshots), predictex-xww (screenshot OCR), predictex-fs6 (resume import). Do NOT close those now; mark superseded only when this is committed.

Open questions for when picked up: do members still mirror picks to FIFA (dual-entry), or is Predictex the sole source? Migration of any already-imported picks? Knockout first-scorer entry UI.
notes: PARTIAL DELIVERY (triaged 2026-06-27): the KNOCKOUT half of the native-entry pivot is DELIVERED + deployed — native in-app R32 entry (5q6 flag v0.11.17, 80k per-fixture unlock v0.11.18, e5o FIFA third-placed resolution v0.11.19), scoreline+booster+first-scorer toggle, lockout-aware, flag-gated. REMAINING (still deferred, 'for later', not committed): the GROUP-STAGE native pivot — members still FIFA-import group picks (xox/4ar bookmarklet) by design; making group entry native + deprecating /import is the uncommitted strategic decision this bead now tracks. Kept OPEN as that umbrella. Sub-items with own beads: cij (KO inline recap), i9k (KO first-scorer import), deferred player-picker (squad rosters absent pre-match).
