title: Cache/scope Standings.leaderboard/0 (recomputed per dashboard load)
description: Predictex.Dashboard.for_player/2 calls Standings.leaderboard/0 on every /predictions page load, which scores all players × all predictions × all completed fixtures just to extract one player's entry and rank — O(league size) per view, no caching. Fine for the current ~15-player league; revisit (ETS cache, per-request memo, or a scoped query) if the league grows or page load gets slow.
notes: DEFERRED 2026-06-24: premature per its own text ('fine for the current ~15-player league; revisit if the league grows or page load gets slow') — trigger has NOT fired. leaderboard/0 is 2 small queries + an in-memory fold over ~15 players; a cross-request cache buys ~nothing at this scale. Worse, making it correct is non-trivial: the cache would have to invalidate from broadcast_change (the 3 result paths) PLUS every prediction writer — the broadcast gap surfaced during scoping (see predictex-broadcast bead, now fixed) was the cache telling us it was premature. REVISIT TRIGGER: league grows materially (e.g. >50 players) OR /predictions / public-board load shows measurable latency in a profile. If revisited: single monotonic version counter bumped by ONE Standings.invalidate/0 from the result + prediction seams (now that prediction writes broadcast, that seam is already wired), NOT per-site cache-busting.

title: Standings.Snapshot: memoize base ranking so in-memory project reuses it
description: Follow-up to the Standings snapshot deepening (architecture-review candidate #3). Once Buzz takes a Standings.Snapshot and the DB loads collapse from ~11/event to 1, the remaining cost is in-memory: scenarios/3 calls Standings.project(snapshot, ...) three times, each re-ranking the whole snapshot from scratch. Memoize the base ranking (or a reusable per-player scored structure) inside the %Standings.Snapshot{} so each project only re-scores the ONE swapped fixture instead of re-ranking everything. Pure, no DB — purely a CPU optimisation on the live/replay hot path. Deferred from the snapshot refactor (which ships the 11->1 DB win first). Low priority: in-memory re-ranks are cheap once the DB round-trips are gone.
notes: DEFERRED 2026-06-24: premature per its own text ('low priority: in-memory re-ranks are cheap once the DB round-trips are gone' — and #3 already shipped that DB win). Scoping found the saving is smaller than it reads (project/4 still runs the full fold; memoization only skips Scoring.score/3 — nanosecond arithmetic at ~15 players × ~50 preds) and the risk lands on the live what-if/buzz path (round-bonus completeness must recompute when the projected fixture completes its round; replace-not-append if the fixture was already completed). REVISIT TRIGGER: the live buzz hot path measurably slows, OR league/fixture counts grow enough that 3× re-scoring per event shows up in a profile. If revisited: keep rank/2 as the from-scratch path (knockout_leaderboard + tests need it) and add an equivalence regression test (memoized project ≡ from-scratch, INCLUDING a round-completed-by-projection case) — that equivalence test is what makes it safe.

title: Match-recap goal breakdown: knockout-ET filtering + own-goal/Type-3 verification (p4o Slice 2 follow-ups)
description:

1. KNOCKOUT-ET FILTERING (gated on predictex-hco): Openfootball.goal_events/1 maps raw goals1/goals2 without excluding ET/penalty goals, while home_goals/away_goals are regulation-only. 2. OWN-GOAL SIDE + TYPE-3 VERIFICATION (gated on first real Type-3 FIFA capture): both decoders attribute a goal to the array it sits in (beneficiary side for own goals). FIFA own-goal (Type 3) array placement is UNVERIFIED — no Type-3 /detail body exists in the repo; real captures live in prod DB. On the first real own-goal capture: verify FIFA's Type-3 placement, and add a Capture.goal_events/1 Type-3(:own_goal) decode assertion to test/predictex/capture_test.exs (currently only Type 1/2 asserted).

3. POLISH (trivial): one-line comment on the __order 2-tuple term-ordering sort in Openfootball.goal_events/1; a test asserting player: nil for an anonymous goal (name absent).

title: Match recap: cards section (deferred until red-card enum verified)

What we know (banked 2026-06-18, tmp/fifa-capture/baseline/, FIFA v3 detail): each team carries a 'Bookings' array parallel to 'Goals', shape {Card:int, Period:int, IdPlayer:str, IdTeam:str, Minute:str, Reason:null}. Player name resolves via the same IdPlayer->Players-list lookup as goals — no new feed.

BLOCKER on shipping: every booking captured so far is Card=1 (yellow). Values 2/3 are INFERRED as second-yellow/straight-red per FIFA convention but NOT empirically confirmed. Reason was null in all samples.

Acceptance (draft):
- A real red/second-yellow booking has been observed and the Card enum is documented from data (not inference).
- Recap shows bookings per player with correct card type (yellow / 2nd-yellow / red) and minute, attributed to the right side.

title: Deferred decision: persist event source IN the capture producer (lossless recording)
description:
DEFERRED ALTERNATIVE to the default pure-subscriber capture model in predictex-rfm. We chose the pure producer/subscriber model (worker publishes; LiveMatch + EventStore are independent PubSub subscribers) for symmetry/simplicity. RISK accepted: Phoenix.PubSub is in-memory + at-most-once, so if the EventStore subscriber is down/crashes on a message, that snapshot is dropped -> a small gap in the authoritative recording that replay (predictex-i1s) reads. At 30s cadence + friends-group scale this is negligible and self-corrects next tick.

TRIGGER to revisit: replay shows gaps/jumps, OR recording integrity becomes important (e.g. monetised, multi-tenant, or analytics that need every snapshot).

WHAT THE DEFERRED OPTION REQUIRES: move persistence INTO the producer — the worker writes the raw body to fifa_captures SYNCHRONOUSLY before broadcasting, so the event source is guaranteed lossless; the standalone EventStore subscriber is then removed/redundant and only reactive consumers (LiveMatch, flourishes) ride PubSub. Localized change to the capture worker; no schema change. Decided 2026-06-17.
notes: Decision context recorded in docs/adr/0001-capture-worker-deployment-isolation.md: 4ya (capture durability) is the complementary 'unplanned-restart' fix to the deferred worker-role deploy split. Land 4ya before any web/worker split so even a rare worker deploy / host reboot is frame-safe. Both deferred until post-28-Jun (worker contract still churning: ius + KnockoutIds).

title: Harden Predictions.save_round_row against direct-API misuse
description: Code-review (a02) flagged two robustness gaps in admin_save_round_predictions/3 that are NOT reachable from the by-player LiveView (which always passes atom-keyed, same-round rows) but would bite a future direct caller: (1) save_round_row stamps the caller-supplied round_id without verifying the fixture belongs to that round, so a cross-round fixture would persist with a wrong denormalized round_id — admin_upsert_prediction/1 and create_prediction/2 both re-derive round_id from the fetched fixture and are immune; (2) the nil/nil and booster_on_blank clauses match atom keys only, so a string-keyed row map falls through and row.fixture_id raises KeyError. Fix: re-derive round_id per row from the fixture, and/or normalise keys at the context boundary. Low priority — current sole caller is safe.
notes:
ARCHITECTURAL SEAM (from 2026-06-18 Gather→Decide→Act review): admin_save_round_predictions/3 (predictions.ex:103-119) uses an inverted validate-then-write seam. It clears all the round's boosters, reduces over rows WRITING each via save_round_row, then scans results for {:error, :booster_on_blank} and Repo.rollback's. The booster-on-blank rule is fully determinable from the input row alone (booster=true + blank scoreline — no DB), yet is discovered post-write and undone. Safe today (the transaction prevents partial writes; correctness-neutral) but the rule can only be exercised by running the whole DB transaction — there is no pure function to test it against. This is also why the LiveView (admin_predictions_live.ex:58-84) must pattern-match on {:error, :booster_on_blank} and summarize/1 the context result instead of validating at the boundary.

FIX (subsumes gap 2 above): extract a pure validate_round_rows(rows) :: :ok | {:error, :booster_on_blank, [fixture_id]} called BEFORE Repo.transaction; only write once it returns :ok. Do key-normalisation (atom/string) inside this pure pre-check so the string-keyed KeyError (gap 2) cannot reach the writer. Gap 1 (re-derive round_id per row from the fixture) stays a writer-side fix. Net: pure decision testable without DB, validation moved to the boundary, all three robustness items closed together.
